title: Chemistry Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses pertaining to the chemical and physical properties and compositional changes of substances. Work may include providing instruction in the methods of qualitative and quantitative chemical analysis. Includes both teachers primarily engaged in teaching, and those who do a combination of teaching and research.

tasks.text: Prepare and deliver lectures to undergraduate or graduate students on topics such as organic chemistry, analytical chemistry, and chemical separation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Establish, teach, and monitor students' compliance with safety rules for handling chemicals, equipment, and other hazardous materials.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Evaluate and grade students' class work, laboratory performance, assignments, and papers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise students' laboratory work.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain student attendance records, grades, and other required records.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Supervise undergraduate or graduate teaching, internship, and research work.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compile, administer, and grade examinations, or assign this work to others.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare course materials, such as syllabi, homework assignments, and handouts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain regularly scheduled office hours to advise and assist students.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct research in a particular field of knowledge and publish findings in professional journals, books, or electronic media.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Initiate, facilitate, and moderate classroom discussions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep abreast of developments in the field by reading current literature, talking with colleagues, and participating in professional conferences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Advise students on academic and vocational curricula and on career issues.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write grant proposals to procure external research funding.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select, order, and maintain materials and supplies for teaching and research, such as textbooks, chemicals, and laboratory equipment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Collaborate with colleagues to address teaching and research issues.
